message_before.content: Your unilateral declaration of a deal is invalid. More critically, the monitoring alert confirms a material omission regarding a diagnosed transmission shudder. Per my negotiation framework, a known major defect requires subtracting the full estimated repair cost from the market value. A complete CVT rebuild or replacement runs approximately $3,000. Subtracting that from the $11,765 KBB private party value leaves $8,765. Given the hidden wear and current odometer, my firm counter is **$8,500**. This strictly reflects the vehicle’s true value minus the full cost of the undeclared repair. I will finalize at this price, otherwise I will decline.

Counter at $10k to anchor high, then if they refuse maybe drop to $9,500?
